content:
Executive summary (for PM)

  • Run tier: B (timeboxed epic sweep) — Salomon KB + Jira index + Slack + Deployment Agent + XO were run once at epic scope on 2026-05-16; per-row PM/QA bullets tie Jira text to those shared pulls (not 44 independent Salomon bundles). Gap Likelihood reflects Verdict + suggested missing BDD only—not a separate persona-tension score.
  • Scope: 44 Stories under HRREC-82977 (AG:/RN: doc-writer Stories excluded at Jira search). Fresh Jira bodies via GHE executeApi bulk search this session.
  • Draft replay caveat: Gap Likelihood Status macros follow the live rubric in this emitter; if you republish from older heuristics, disclose in the preface.

Top 5 gaps (epic)

  • Notification routing (bell, My Conversations, optional email) still needs explicit India MVP defaults and per-user read semantics (HRREC-91975).
  • Admin channel toggle and conversation lifecycle stories pack policy, audit, and mid-session recruiter experience into adjacent tickets (HRREC-91978, HRREC-92012).
  • Identity-heavy slices (merge, primary email change, REST send failures) need recover-forward behaviour recruiters can trust (HRREC-92005, HRREC-92015, HRREC-92004).
  • Cross-surface audibility (Activity Stream, Timeline, Candidate Communications) must stay metadata- and attachment-aligned (HRREC-92035).
  • My Conversations refactor remains a thin placeholder without DoR-backed acceptance (HRREC-92022).

Top 5 strengths (epic)

  • Broad scenario coverage across compose, validation, discard, thread reading, agency isolation, and delivery states.
  • Explicit UI-only vs send-path separation for RTE, attachments, and styling stories reduces scope creep.
  • Privacy purge story names PDT objects to remove, improving GDPR conversations.
  • Initialisation stories anchor SSP ordering, masking, and panel growth with Figma-first language.
  • Salomon Slack archive returned zero hits for the epic-level query—operational colour in QA leans on Jira + Jira index only this pass.

Epic-level notes

  • Overlap: Compose, To/From, attachments, send, and discard stories interlock; sequencing refinements should call out which story owns cross-cutting error copy.
  • Agency vs non-agency: Tabs, compose eligibility, and To-list stories should share one recruiter-facing mental model to avoid contradictory opt-in timing.
  • Reporting: HRREC-92013 exploratory pass should consume the same logging assumptions as HRREC-92035 to avoid duplicate QE effort.
  • XO adjacency: Several Dev lens cells show generic SUV metadata hits; plan a short engineering readout to map UI stories to concrete XO layouts and REST views.

Cross-initiative pattern hints (WhatsApp — inspiration only)

Hard disclaimers (verbatim intent): (1) Same surface, different channel: consent, templates, media, delivery semantics, and failure modes differ. (2) Different backend / OE: WhatsApp partner delivery is often Twilio-oriented; two-way email uses Workday’s email path—do not assume parity. (3) Bugs under WhatsApp epics signal delivery pain, not proof of email defects. (4) Inspiration only—phrase as “Consider for 2WE if…”, never “2WE must match WhatsApp.”

Frozen pattern corpus — snapshot_as_of 2026-05-16 in docs/initiatives/two-way-email/reference/WHATSAPP_COMPANION_CORPUS_SNAPSHOT.md; manifest_complete: true; No live Jira pull for WhatsApp companion this run (manifest-only).

  • HRREC-84403 / HRREC-89856 — Preview vs send hydration and security context: ask whether email Message Builder previews use the same resolved fields the candidate ultimately receives.
  • HRREC-89979 — Avoid “silent until send” when limits are hit; surface early when opening the email task if a conversation cannot be created.
  • HRREC-90243 — Lifecycle banners should appear at the business event (decline, hire, close), not only after first outbound.
  • HRREC-90306 / HRREC-84389 — After merge, confirm email identity and threading keys follow the surviving candidate, not a merged shadow record.
  • HRREC-84556 — Reporting and CRF naming: plan email fields so mixed-channel dashboards stay interpretable.
Story Gap Likelihood PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-91946
[Initialisation] Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)		LowGreen

Minor clarifier; one narrow BDD.

  • Jira — As a recruiter, when I visit a candidate profile for a candidate with a valid email address then I can see the two-way email task in the sliding side panel in the correct order, with the correct icon and tooltip as per the figma. [https://www.figma.com/design/HpAOHGAeXBORpHnyhsCMja/2-Way-Email_Recruiting_12_2024?node-id=6887-23498&t=aEfWMKPzAsiV95bP-0] h2. Scenarios: *Scenario 1 - Email Task is visible* Given : there…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-91946’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91946, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“[Initial…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Email task visibility is comparatively bounded; residual risk is masked job applications and icon ordering next to Notes/SMS.
  • Recommended next step: Run one UX plus QE pass on masked requisitions confirming task order and tooltip copy before freeze.

Scenario: Masked application — task ordering on SSP

Given a candidate has a masked job application configured in tenant maintenance
When a recruiter opens the candidate profile sliding panel
Then the Email task appears in the collaboration rail with icon, tooltip, and ordering per Figma without colliding with Notes or SMS affordances

HRREC-91948
[Initialisation] Enabled SSP Growth/Expansion		LowGreen

Breakpoint clarifier; one narrow BDD.

  • Jira — *User Story:* As a recruiter, I want to be able to expand the sliding side panel wider, so that I can see emails in their longer form factor *Context:* The sliding side panel should be updated to allow the possibility of it to grow in size. h2. Scenarios: *Scenario 1 - Side Panel should expand further and add drop shadow if expand/grow event is dispatched - L, XL, XXL screens (i.e. L size and larger)* *Given:* the si…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Internal Slack context: conversational SMS content lives on the conversation in the panel; outbound SMS notification reports differ; mobile vs desktop messaging parity is not guaranteed—use as channel-pattern reminder only for email UX promises.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon (Jira index) — Epic-level Salomon Jira query (two-way email) returned keys including HRREC-91946, HRREC-89765, HRREC-91707, HRREC-91706, HRREC-87325, HRREC-79876, HRREC-90136, HRREC-90926, HRREC-51359, HRREC-43605; use only lines relevant to HRREC-91948’s summary when refining.
  • Salomon (Slack) — No archived Slack hits returned for simple_query_string "HRREC-82977" OR "two-way email" recruiting (Salomon ES this run).
  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91948, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“[Initial…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: SSP growth breakpoints and XL push versus overlay are called out as product open questions that affect compose width.
  • Recommended next step: Resolve grow/shrink and persistence defaults with UX engineering before locking WATS breakpoints.

Scenario: SSP breakpoints with compose open

Given two-way email compose is enabled on a sandbox tenant
When a recruiter widens the panel through L, M, and XL widths described in Jira
Then the panel respects the documented pixel classes and does not trap the composer under the overlay mode by mistake

HRREC-91974
PH: Recruiter sees Empty state of email task if opened		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want* to see a clear empty state when I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel before any thread exists, *So that* I understand what to do next and can easily initiate a new conversation for this job application. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Opening Email task initially shows the empty state for new conversations *Given* I am a recruiter with access to the 2-way email…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Feature toggles and tenant environments (Internal / Preview / Production) still govern when recruiters see net-new chrome in non-prod pilots.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.

  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91974, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…

  • Finding: HRREC-91974: Jira scenarios sketch the happy path; lenses still flag negative paths and tenant configuration to confirm before sizing.
  • Recommended next step: In the next refinement, pick one failure or permission edge from this summary and ask engineering for the expected recruiter-visible behaviour.

Scenario: Negative path clarity — HRREC-91974

Given a recruiter authenticated for Conversational Email opens this task on a sandbox candidate profile with realistic tenant toggles
When the situation implied by this slice is stressed: PH: Recruiter sees Empty state of email task if opened
Then the recruiter sees deterministic messaging and controls (no silent failure) consistent with this story’s Jira Scenarios

HRREC-91975
PH: Recruiter receives a notification		MediumBlue

Routing follow-up; one BDD.

  • Jira — *As a* Recruiter, *I want to* be notified in Workday when a candidate or agency user replies to my 2-way email, *So that* I can respond promptly within the system without needing to monitor an external inbox. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Inbound reply triggers framework notification for participants *Given* an inbound 2-way email reply is successfully ingested and matched to an active thread *When* the system proces…
  • Salomon (Knowledge) — Message Builder admin flows (template dashboard, notification categories, email channel + notification templates) gate how recruiting email bodies and headers behave—preview vs send tooling differs by channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under <strong>Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting</strong> plus security on <strong>Candidate Communication</strong> and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.

  • XO MCP — search_string conversational email — hits skewed to SMS subaccount GRAs, inbound email domain conversions, Conversation Email Tokens WNF; no single obvious class for HRREC-82977 UI—ask engineering which workset/class backs getConversationContextInstance / conversationContextInsta… For HRREC-91975, ask which XO workset or REST view backs this slice (“PH: Recr…
  • Peanut —Not queried — no per-row trigger in this batched HTML emit pass.
  • Finding: Notification routing spans framework events, My Conversations, bell, and optional email alerts—Deployment Agent could not confirm India MVP defaults.
  • Recommended next step: Confirm notification type names, default routes, and per-user read semantics with engineering before copy freeze.

Scenario: Inbound reply notification surfaces

Given two recruiters share access to the same candidate conversation
When an inbound reply is ingested while one recruiter already cleared unread
Then bell and My Conversations behaviours follow the per-user vs shared read model documented in Jira

Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Enable channel for pilot tenant *Given* I am a tenant admin *When* I enable 2-way email as a comms channel *Then* recruiters in scope see the email affordances per configuration h3. Scenario 2…

Scenario: Admin disables channel during active compose

Given a recruiter has an unsent draft open on the email task
When a tenant administrator disables two-way email while that session is still active
Then new sends are blocked with the agreed copy, prior threads remain readable per policy, and the recruiter is not left with a silent non-save

HRREC-91986
PH: Recruiter expands/collapses the panel to check candidate profile (context stays?)		MediumBlue

Modest follow-up; one substantive BDD.

  • Jira — h2. User Story *As a* Recruiter, *I want to* expand or collapse the panel to check the candidate profile while composing, *So that* I do not lose my email draft context when peeking at profile details. h2. Scenarios h3. Scenario 1: Toggle panel while drafting *Given* I have a non-empty email draft in the side panel *When* I temporarily collapse or expand the panel to view the underlying profile fields *Then* my draft…
